food_subgroup: Rotisserie chicken thigh in pita flatbread with garlic toum sauce, tahini, pickles, tomato, and lettuce. Singapore restaurant portion ~280g per wrap.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03311527 covers shawarma as a category; no specific chicken shawarma wrap node exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for chicken shawarma wrap as of 2026-04.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Singapore restaurant chicken shawarma wrap weighing approximately 280g, comprising 60g pita bread, 120g marinated rotisserie chicken thigh, 45ml garlic toum sauce, and the remainder as pickled vegetables, tomato, and lettuce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA SR Legacy — Chicken thigh (roasted, skin removed), pita bread, tahini, garlic, vegetable oil components; composite estimate for assembled wrap
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Chicken shawarma wrap is a composite dish. Per-100g values derived from USDA SR Legacy component data for roasted chicken thigh (FDC ID 171477), pita bread (FDC ID 174924), tahini (FDC ID 169560), and garlic (FDC ID 169230), combined with a traditional Lebanese toum recipe estimate (garlic emulsified in vegetable oil). Garlic toum is not available as a standalone entry in USDA Branded Foods; fat and sodium contribution estimated from traditional recipe proportions. Composite weighted per 280g serving then expressed per 100g. Confidence: moderate (0.7).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — pita bread GI ~57; composite wrap GI estimated ~60 accounting for fat and protein moderation from garlic toum and chicken
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pita bread GI ~57 (medium) per University of Sydney GI Database. The assembled wrap GI estimated at ~60 (medium) because the high fat content of garlic toum (~14g fat per wrap, MUFA/PUFA dominant) and the protein content of chicken thigh (~28g per wrap) moderately attenuate postprandial glucose response relative to pita alone. GL per 280g serving = (GI 60 × 42g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 25.2. This is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement of chicken shawarma wrap was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 1,075mg per 280g wrap (53.8% of the 2,000mg daily limit), driven primarily by garlic toum sauce (~500mg Na), pickled vegetables (~300mg Na), tahini sauce (~150mg Na), and chicken marinade (~125mg Na).
hypertension.guidance: Hypertensive patients should request reduced garlic toum sauce (halving from 45ml to 20ml saves approximately 220mg sodium) and avoid pickled vegetables; note that toum tastes like garlic cream rather than salt, so patients may not perceive the sodium load without guidance.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic load of 25.2 per wrap driven by 42g available carbohydrate from pita bread (GI ~60 medium); partially mitigated by 28g protein and 24g fat per wrap which attenuate postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should eat the chicken and sauce components first before the pita (carbohydrate sequencing reduces postprandial glucose by 15–25%) and may request wholemeal or thin flatbread to further reduce the glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is low at 1.5g per 100g (4.2g per wrap); fat profile is predominantly MUFA and PUFA from garlic toum vegetable oil emulsion, which is cardioprotective; however, sodium at ~1,075mg per wrap is a significant cardiovascular concern.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The favourable MUFA/PUFA fat profile from garlic toum is a positive factor for cardiovascular health, but the high sodium content (~1,075mg per wrap) requires portion control of sauces and pickled condiments for patients with cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Garlic toum provides allicin (anti-inflammatory) and MUFA/PUFA; chicken thigh provides vitamin B6, selenium, and niacin; high sodium (~1,075mg) is a concern for patients on corticosteroids.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis on corticosteroids should limit sodium intake by reducing toum and pickled vegetables; the anti-inflammatory properties of garlic and the MUFA/PUFA fat profile are beneficial.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Shawarma is a Levantine and Turkish rotisserie chicken wrap widely popular in Singapore's Middle Eastern restaurant scene. Marinated chicken thigh is stacked on a vertical spit (the Arabic 'shawarma' derives from Turkish 'çevirme' meaning turning or rotating), slow-roasted, then thinly shaved and wrapped in pita flatbread with garlic toum sauce, tahini, pickled cucumbers, pickled turnip, tomato, and lettuce. The Singapore restaurant portion weighs approximately 280g per wrap. Per wrap: approximately 549 kcal, 28g protein, 24g fat (predominantly MUFA and PUFA from garlic toum vegetable oil emulsion), 42g carbohydrate, GI ~60 (medium), GL ~25.2, and approximately 1,075mg sodium. The garlic toum sauce is the primary fat source (~14g fat per wrap) and the primary sodium source (~500mg Na per wrap); it is perceived as a garlic cream sauce rather than a salty condiment, meaning patients often underestimate its sodium contribution. Rotisserie cooking renders approximately 30% of the fat from the chicken thigh. The dish is halal and dairy-free. Key clinical considerations: high sodium load (hypertension, cardiovascular disease); moderate glycemic load manageable with carbohydrate sequencing (Type 2 Diabetes); favourable MUFA/PUFA fat profile. Available at Zaatar W Zeit, Al Shams, Sofra Turkish, and Cedar Lebanese restaurants in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values derived from USDA SR Legacy component data for roasted chicken thigh, pita bread, tahini, and garlic, combined with a traditional Lebanese toum recipe estimate. Garlic toum is not available as a standalone entry in USDA Branded Foods or Singapore HPB database; fat and sodium contributions are estimated from traditional recipe proportions. The composite assembly introduces moderate uncertainty. Overall confidence 0.7 (medium).
notes: water_g set to -1: moisture data for the assembled chicken shawarma wrap composite is not directly available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as a single entry; individual component moisture values were not aggregated due to uncertainty in final moisture after rotisserie cooking and assembly. monounsaturated_fat_g (4.2g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.1g) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for roasted chicken thigh and vegetable oil (sunflower/canola blend typical for toum); these values are approximate. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy for the component ingredients. Garlic toum is not in USDA Branded Foods; sodium and fat contributions estimated from traditional Lebanese toum recipe (garlic emulsified in vegetable oil with lemon juice and salt). GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on pita bread GI ~57 from the University of Sydney GI Database, adjusted upward slightly for the composite wrap; no direct GI measurement of chicken shawarma wrap was found. GL per 280g serving = (60 × 42g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 25.2. This record covers the Singapore restaurant portion (~280g wrap). Sodium per wrap ~1,075mg = 384mg per 100g × 2.8.
